g.data-narration: Introduction to clustering algorithms including partitional, density-based, and grid-based methods
Clustering groups similar data points together based on their characteristics and similarities
Clustering is used for customer segmentation, image analysis, and anomaly detection
Partitional clustering divides data into K disjoint clusters by minimizing within-cluster variance
g.text: Divides data into K disjoint clusters, minimizing within-cluster variance.
Each data point belongs to exactly one cluster.

g.data-narration: K-Means iteratively assigns points to nearest centroid and updates centroids as cluster mean
g.text: Step 2: Assign each point to nearest centroid
Step 3: Update centroids as cluster mean
J = Sum_i Sum_x in C_i ||x - mu_i||^2

g.data-narration: K-Means converges iteratively, assigning points to clusters until centroids stabilize
g.text: • Complexity: O(n x K x I x d)

g.data-narration: K-Medoids uses actual data points as cluster centers, making it robust to outliers
g.text: Uses medoids (actual data points) instead of centroids - more robust to outliers

g.data-narration: Hierarchical clustering builds a dendrogram showing cluster relationships at different levels
g.text: Builds a hierarchy of clusters using dendrogram - no need to specify K

g.data-narration: Density-based methods find clusters of arbitrary shape based on point density
g.text: Identifies clusters based on density of data points - can find arbitrary shapes
• Core Points: MinPts neighbors within eps
• Reachability: Within eps from core point

g.data-narration: DBSCAN uses epsilon and MinPts parameters to identify dense regions as clusters
g.text: Parameters: eps = neighborhood radius, MinPts = minimum points
2. If point has >= MinPts neighbors - Core

g.data-narration: Grid-based clustering divides space into cells and merges dense adjacent cells
g.text: Divides data space into grid cells - efficient for high-dimensional data
3. Merge dense adjacent cells into clusters

g.data-narration: Each clustering algorithm has strengths: K-Means for spheres, DBSCAN for arbitrary shapes
Clustering applies to customer segmentation, image processing, fraud detection, and document organization
Choose clustering method based on cluster shape, outlier presence, scalability, and dimensionality
